title: callitriche aquatica subsp. pedunculata
intro: Callitriche aquatica subsp. pedunculata, also known as aquatic springwort, is a subspecies of aquatic plant belonging to the Callitriche genus, characterized by its delicate, hair-like leaves and stems that thrive in freshwater environments. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its antispasmodic and expectorant effects. Consuming aquatic springwort has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these findings. Aquatic springwort is often prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be dried or used as a fresh addition to salads, smoothies, and other recipes.
